text: Writing clean code is essential for any developer. Readable code is easy for others—and your future self—to understand. When naming variables and functions, use clear, descriptive names that explain their purpose. This helps people follow your logic without reading long comments. Also, keep each function focused on one specific task. Short, simple functions are easier to test and fix if something goes wrong. By following these habits, you improve your code's maintainability, making it simpler to update and add new features later. Good coding practices save time and reduce frustration.
